2605.15077.title: Concurrency without Model Changes: Future-based Asynchronous Function Calling for LLMs
2605.15077.paper_summary: Function calling, also known as tool use, is a core capability of modern LLM agents but is typically constrained by synchronous execution semantics. Under these semantics, LLM decoding is blocked until each function call completes, resulting in increasing end-to-end latency. In this work, we introduce AsyncFC, a pure execution-layer framework that decouples LLM decoding from function execution, enabling overlap between model decoding and function execution as well as inter-function parallelism when dependencies permit. AsyncFC layers over existing models and unmodified function implementations, requiring no fine-tuning or changes to the standard synchronous function-calling protocol. Across standard function-calling benchmarks and adapted software engineering benchmarks, AsyncFC significantly reduces end-to-end task completion time while preserving task accuracy. Furthermore, these results reveal that LLMs possess a native capability to reason over symbolic futures that represent unresolved execution results, enabling an asynchronous paradigm for model-tool interaction.
2605.15077.paper_summary_zh: 功能調用，也被稱為工具使用，是現代 LLM 代理的核心能力，但通常受到同步執行語義的限制。根據這些語義，LLM 解碼在每次函數調用完成之前會被阻塞，導致端到端延遲增加。在這項工作中，我們介紹了 AsyncFC，一個純執行層框架，將 LLM 解碼與函數執行解耦，實現模型解碼與函數執行之間的重疊，以及在依賴關係允許的情況下的函數間並行性。AsyncFC 層疊於現有模型和未經修改的函數實現之上，無需微調或更改標準的同步函數調用協議。在標準函數調用基準測試和改編的軟件工程基準測試中，AsyncFC 顯著減少了端到端任務完成時間，同時保持了任務準確性。此外，這些結果揭示了 LLM 具備對表示未解決執行結果的符號未來進行推理的原生能力，從而實現了模型與工具交互的非同步範式。
2605.15077.author: Guangyu Feng et.al.
2605.15077.authors: Guangyu Feng, Huanzhi Mao, Prabal Dutta, Joseph E. Gonzalez
